title: Spoke Capabilities Registry — hub-base + local override, self-documenting, runner-executed
one_liner: Formalize a per-spoke capabilities registry (a self-documenting functional manual of runnable commands with validity windows, run triggers, and tracking) that mirrors the hub-to-spoke pattern: hub holds a base set, each spoke extends or overrides by id. A working prototype runs in track-prompt-lab + hub; the framework should design the canonical version and publish a teaching that tells spokes how to adopt or adapt.
why_this_matters: There is no per-spoke record of what scripts a spoke can run, how long they stay valid, when they auto-run, or where they are documented. That knowledge is scattered across basher.json (environment/restore only), advisors/registry.json (advisor scheduling only), and tribal memory. The first concrete need was centralizing the WAI Track prompt so spokes stop drifting; that generalized into a reusable capabilities mechanism. Framework ownership matters because this is fleet-wide infrastructure: the framework is the blueprint, and a prototype built in one spoke must be canonicalized centrally so all spokes adopt one consistent shape.

prototype_reference.summary: A working reference implementation exists. The framework may keep, reshape, or replace it — it is a blueprint, not a mandate.

files_built.hub/tools/capability_runner.py: Generic runner: merges hub-base + spoke-local registries (local wins by id), runs capabilities whose run.trigger matches and run.auto is true, writes a self-documenting effective manifest + append-only run log, auto-creates an idempotent renewal lug when valid_until has passed. Always exits 0 so it can never block spoke entry.
files_built.WAI-Spoke/capabilities.json: Per-spoke local registry that extends/overrides base by id (track-prompt-lab seeded with track-prompt-publish [author_only] and dev-server).
files_built.hub/tools/track_prompt_publish.py: Author spoke publishes its validated track prompt to the hub canonical location (the first capability's executable).
files_built.hub/tools/track_prompt_spoke_sync.py: Consumer spoke pulls the canonical track prompt from the hub; SHA-compares and is a no-op when current.
files_built.basher/wai-enter.sh: Canonical entry wrapper now calls capability_runner.py --trigger on-entry in the spoke prep block (skipped on -c continue mode). All spoke wrappers inherit it.
files_built.hub/hub-registry.json: track-prompt-lab marked track_prompt_role: author so it publishes rather than pulls.

capability_schema.name: human label
capability_schema.usage: how to invoke it manually + when it auto-runs (self-documenting)
capability_schema.command: shell command with {hub_path} and {spoke_path} substitution tokens
capability_schema.valid_until: YYYY-MM-DD — past this the runner skips and files a renewal lug

run.auto: bool — whether the runner executes it automatically

capability_schema.skip_if: simple `field == value` / `field != value` predicate evaluated against the spoke's hub-registry entry

behaviors_proven: skip_if predicate (track-prompt-sync skipped on author spoke)
valid_until expiry -> idempotent renewal lug with full PEV schema
verified end-to-end: published track prompt v0.40 to hub, synced to consumer spoke 'minder' on entry

decide: Canonical location and ownership of the base registry (WAI-Hub/reference/ vs elsewhere) and the per-spoke local file (WAI-Spoke/capabilities.json vs folding into basher.json — note basher.json is restore-focused and has an in-flight enrich lug).
The authoritative capability schema and required vs optional fields.
The skip_if / activation predicate grammar (keep the simple field==value form or formalize a richer one).
Run-tracking format and whether it should feed the gardener/nightly lifecycle and PathGraph.
Validity/retirement policy: renewal-lug-on-expiry (prototype) vs a different lifecycle, and default validity windows.
Whether the runner lives in hub/tools (prototype) or is framework-distributed.
framework_mandate.constraint: Must preserve the hub-base + local-override pattern and the self-documenting (description + usage) requirement — these are the two locked design decisions from the originating session.

adaptation_contract.requirement: Per the originating intent: the framework may serve this differently than the prototype. If it does, the published teaching MUST tell adopting spokes how to adapt, not merely that a new mechanism exists.
teaching_must_specify: Prototype -> canonical mapping: any file moves, renames, or relocations (e.g. if capabilities.json moves or merges into basher.json).
Schema migration: field renames, additions, removals, and how to transform an existing prototype capabilities.json into the canonical shape.
Runner invocation changes: if wai-enter.sh should call something other than hub/tools/capability_runner.py.
What track-prompt-lab and the hub specifically must change to converge from the prototype to canonical (this spoke and the hub are already running the prototype).
Idempotency guidance so re-adoption does not duplicate capabilities or renewal lugs.
adaptation_contract.back_reference: On adoption, spokes (including track-prompt-lab) reconcile their local capabilities.json against the canonical schema using the teaching's migration steps.

perceive.current_state: Prototype live in track-prompt-lab + hub; no framework-canonical spec or teaching yet. No other spoke has adopted (consumers had no local track prompt before; minder now seeded via the prototype runner).

steps: 1. Review the prototype reference implementation and behaviors_proven.
2. Decide the canonical schema, locations, predicate grammar, tracking format, and retirement policy (see framework_mandate.decide), preserving the two locked constraints.
3. Write the canonical capabilities-registry spec document (Man Lug System: bytype/spec/, index in WAI-SpecIndex.jsonl).
4. Define the runner contract and where it is distributed from.
5. Publish a framework teaching that (a) introduces the mechanism and (b) satisfies the adaptation_contract.teaching_must_specify migration items so spokes can adopt or adapt.
6. Specify the seed path: how a spoke with no local capabilities.json gets one (the runner already tolerates its absence, so adoption is incremental and non-breaking).

done_when: Canonical capabilities-registry spec exists and is indexed in WAI-SpecIndex.jsonl
A framework teaching is published in the teachings repo
The teaching includes explicit prototype->canonical migration steps (adaptation_contract satisfied)
track-prompt-lab and the hub have a documented path to converge from prototype to canonical
Hub-base + local-override and self-documenting (description + usage) are preserved in the canonical schema

acceptance_criteria: Canonical schema preserves hub-base + local-override merged by id (local wins)
Every capability carries description + usage (self-documenting manual)
Validity window with a defined retirement/renewal policy
Run triggers expressive enough for on-entry, on-demand, scheduled, and on-event, reusing advisors/registry.json scheduling vocabulary where possible
Published teaching tells adopting spokes how to adapt if the canonical differs from the prototype, with concrete migration steps
